title: lithocarpus lappaceus
intro: Lithocarpus lappaceus, also known as the California nutmeg or Tanbark oak, is a species of tree in the beech family Fagaceae. It is native to the Pacific coast of North America, particularly in the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Lithocarpus lappaceus is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and can be consumed as a tea or used topically for its therapeutic benefits.
